title: ChordPlayReceipt
description: Machine-readable receipt emitted by tools/chord_play.ts after attempting to verify a chord's claims. Sibling to the human-readable .md receipt-chord.

chord_id.description: ID of the chord that was played. Matches the chord's `id` field.

chord_path.description: Repository-relative path to the chord file.

verdict.description: Overall outcome of the play attempt. 'passed' = all expected_after_running claims satisfied; 'failed' = some claim not satisfied (and not trial-mode); 'dormant' = chord ran but state unchanged (no work was needed); 'promoted' = chord transitioned from draft to active; 'trial-reverted' = trial-mode chord reverted via git stash drop after failed claim.

fingerprint.description: SHA-256 of the chord's canonical content at the moment of play. Lets the receipt point unambiguously at the exact chord text that was verified.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when the play was performed.

delta.description: Map of claim-key → resulting value, for each claim in expected_after_running. Allows downstream tooling to see exactly what the substrate state was after the play.

commands_run.description: Shell commands that were executed during the play (typically from chord.suggested_commands).

trial_reverted.description: True if the play was in TRIAL mode and the work was rolled back via git stash drop because the verdict was 'trial-reverted'.

pre.description: Snapshot of relevant substrate state before the play.

post.description: Snapshot of relevant substrate state after the play.

errors.description: Any errors encountered during play (command failures, parse errors, etc).
